003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.function;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025/**
026 * A {@link Supplier} whose value may be null, with Optional-like null-safe query methods.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * This interface extends {@link Supplier} and provides convenience methods for working with potentially null values,
030 * similar to {@link Optional}. The key difference is that this interface is lazy - the value is only computed when
031 * {@link #get()} is called, and the null-safe methods operate on that computed value.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
034 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
035 *    <li>Extends Supplier - can be used anywhere a Supplier is expected
036 *    <li>Null-safe API - provides isPresent(), isEmpty(), map(), orElse(), etc.
037 *    <li>Lazy evaluation - value is only computed when get() is called
038 *    <li>Null-safe - handles null values gracefully
039 * </ul>
040 *
041 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
042 * <p class='bjava'>
043 *    <jc>// Create from a supplier</jc>
044 *    NullableSupplier&lt;String&gt; <jv>supplier</jv> = NullableSupplier.<jsm>of</jsm>(() -&gt; <js>"value"</js>);
045 *
046 *    <jc>// Check if value is present</jc>
047 *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>supplier</jv>.isPresent()) {
048 *       String <jv>value</jv> = <jv>supplier</jv>.get();
049 *    }
050 *
051 *    <jc>// Map the value</jc>
052 *    NullableSupplier&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>length</jv> = <jv>supplier</jv>.map(String::length);
053 *
054 *    <jc>// Get value or default</jc>
055 *    String <jv>result</jv> = <jv>supplier</jv>.orElse(<js>"default"</js>);
056 * </p>
057 *
058 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
059 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Supplier} - Base functional interface
060 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Optional} - Java's Optional class
061 * </ul>
062 *
063 * @param <T> The type of value supplied by this supplier.
064 */
065@FunctionalInterface
066@SuppressWarnings({
067   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
068})
069public interface NullableSupplier<T> extends Supplier<T> {
070
071   /** Argument name constant for assertArgNotNull. */
072   static final String ARG_action = "action";
073   /** Argument name constant for assertArgNotNull. */
074   static final String ARG_emptyAction = "emptyAction";
075   /** Argument name constant for assertArgNotNull. */
076   static final String ARG_exceptionSupplier = "exceptionSupplier";
077   /** Argument name constant for assertArgNotNull. */
078   static final String ARG_mapper = "mapper";
079   /** Argument name constant for assertArgNotNull. */
080   static final String ARG_other = "other";
081   /** Argument name constant for assertArgNotNull. */
082   static final String ARG_predicate = "predicate";
083   /** Argument name constant for assertArgNotNull. */
084   static final String ARG_supplier = "supplier";
085
086   /**
087    * Creates a NullableSupplier from a Supplier.
088    *
089    * @param <T> The value type.
090    * @param supplier The supplier. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
091    * @return A new NullableSupplier instance.
092    */
093   public static <T> NullableSupplier<T> of(Supplier<T> supplier) {
094      assertArgNotNull(ARG_supplier, supplier);
095      return supplier::get;
096   }
097
098   /**
099    * Creates a NullableSupplier that always returns the specified value.
100    *
101    * @param <T> The value type.
102    * @param value The value to return. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
103    * @return A new NullableSupplier instance.
104    */
105   public static <T> NullableSupplier<T> ofNullable(T value) {
106      return () -> value;
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Creates an empty NullableSupplier that always returns <jk>null</jk>.
111    *
112    * @param <T> The value type.
113    * @return A new NullableSupplier instance that always returns <jk>null</jk>.
114    */
115   public static <T> NullableSupplier<T> empty() {
116      return () -> null;
117   }
118
119   /**
120    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the supplied value is not <jk>null</jk>.
121    *
122    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the supplied value is not <jk>null</jk>.
123    */
124   default boolean isPresent() {
125      return nn(get());
126   }
127
128   /**
129    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the supplied value is <jk>null</jk>.
130    *
131    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the supplied value is <jk>null</jk>.
132    */
133   default boolean isEmpty() {
134      return ! isPresent();
135   }
136
137   /**
138    * If a value is present, applies the provided mapping function to it and returns a NullableSupplier describing the result.
139    *
140    * @param <U> The type of the result of the mapping function.
141    * @param mapper A mapping function to apply to the value, if present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
142    * @return A NullableSupplier describing the result of applying a mapping function to the value of this NullableSupplier, if a value is present, otherwise an empty NullableSupplier.
143    */
144   default <U> NullableSupplier<U> map(Function<? super T, ? extends U> mapper) {
145      assertArgNotNull(ARG_mapper, mapper);
146      return () -> {
147         T value = get();
148         return nn(value) ? mapper.apply(value) : null;
149      };
150   }
151
152   /**
153    * If a value is present, returns the result of applying the given NullableSupplier-bearing mapping function to the value, otherwise returns an empty NullableSupplier.
154    *
155    * @param <U> The type parameter to the NullableSupplier returned by the mapping function.
156    * @param mapper A mapping function to apply to the value, if present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
157    * @return The result of applying a NullableSupplier-bearing mapping function to the value of this NullableSupplier, if a value is present, otherwise an empty NullableSupplier.
158    */
159   default <U> NullableSupplier<U> flatMap(Function<? super T, ? extends NullableSupplier<? extends U>> mapper) {
160      assertArgNotNull(ARG_mapper, mapper);
161      return () -> {
162         T value = get();
163         if (nn(value)) {
164            NullableSupplier<? extends U> result = mapper.apply(value);
165            return result != null ? result.get() : null;
166         }
167         return null;
168      };
169   }
170
171   /**
172    * If a value is present, and the value matches the given predicate, returns a NullableSupplier describing the value, otherwise returns an empty NullableSupplier.
173    *
174    * @param predicate A predicate to apply to the value, if present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
175    * @return A NullableSupplier describing the value of this NullableSupplier if a value is present and the value matches the given predicate, otherwise an empty NullableSupplier.
176    */
177   default NullableSupplier<T> filter(Predicate<? super T> predicate) {
178      assertArgNotNull(ARG_predicate, predicate);
179      return () -> {
180         T value = get();
181         return (nn(value) && predicate.test(value)) ? value : null;
182      };
183   }
184
185   /**
186    * If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise returns <jk>other</jk>.
187    *
188    * @param other The value to be returned if there is no value present. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
189    * @return The value, if present, otherwise <jk>other</jk>.
190    */
191   default T orElse(T other) {
192      T value = get();
193      return nn(value) ? value : other;
194   }
195
196   /**
197    * If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise returns the result produced by the supplying function.
198    *
199    * @param other A {@link Supplier} whose result is returned if no value is present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
200    * @return The value, if present, otherwise the result of <jk>other.get()</jk>.
201    */
202   default T orElseGet(Supplier<? extends T> other) {
203      assertArgNotNull(ARG_other, other);
204      T value = get();
205      return nn(value) ? value : other.get();
206   }
207
208   /**
209    * If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise throws an exception produced by the exception supplying function.
210    *
211    * @param <X> Type of the exception to be thrown.
212    * @param exceptionSupplier The supplying function that produces an exception to be thrown. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
213    * @return The value, if present.
214    * @throws X If no value is present.
215    */
216   default <X extends Throwable> T orElseThrow(Supplier<? extends X> exceptionSupplier) throws X {
217      assertArgNotNull(ARG_exceptionSupplier, exceptionSupplier);
218      T value = get();
219      if (nn(value))
220         return value;
221      throw exceptionSupplier.get();
222   }
223
224   /**
225    * If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise does nothing.
226    *
227    * @param action The action to be performed, if a value is present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
228    */
229   default void ifPresent(Consumer<? super T> action) {
230      assertArgNotNull(ARG_action, action);
231      T value = get();
232      if (nn(value))
233         action.accept(value);
234   }
235
236   /**
237    * If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise performs the given empty-based action.
238    *
239    * @param action The action to be performed, if a value is present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
240    * @param emptyAction The empty-based action to be performed, if no value is present. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
241    */
242   default void ifPresentOrElse(Consumer<? super T> action, Runnable emptyAction) {
243      assertArgNotNull(ARG_action, action);
244      assertArgNotNull(ARG_emptyAction, emptyAction);
245      T value = get();
246      if (nn(value))
247         action.accept(value);
248      else
249         emptyAction.run();
250   }
251
252   /**
253    * Converts this NullableSupplier to an {@link Optional}.
254    *
255    * @return An Optional containing the value if present, otherwise an empty Optional.
256    */
257   default Optional<T> toOptional() {
258      return o(get());
259   }
260}
